description:
You are a Performance Engineer investigating random application latency spikes. Standard monitoring dashboards show average CPU usage hovering safely around 40%, but you suspect the underlying workloads are actually 'thrashing'—rapidly spiking and dropping between measurement intervals. To prove this, create a Graphite dashboard named 'Workload Burstiness Index' containing three graphs that visualize the magnitude of these oscillations.

The 'Compute Thrashing' graph must display the raw metric `servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization` and a 'Volatility Index' created by applying `derivative()`, `absolute()`, and `movingAverage()` (window of 10) to the raw metric, aliased as 'CPU Volatility'.

The 'Database Thrashing' graph must display `servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization` and a 'Volatility Index' using the same function sequence (derivative, absolute, movingAverage 10) applied to the database metric, aliased as 'DB Volatility'.

The 'Network Burstiness' graph must display `servers.ec2_instance_1.network.bytes_in` and a 'Volatility Index' using the same function sequence applied to the network metric, aliased as 'Network Volatility'.

Save the completed dashboard, ensuring all dashboard names, graph titles, and aliases match the requirements exactly.
